instrument_family: ancient Greek yoke lyre / chelys-inspired string instrument
design_goal: Create an original historically informed 13-string Greek yoke lyre with top-down SolidWorks parametric control, family-aware variants, Japanese joinery influence, torii-inspired yoke language, Broinwood-style inlay, ergonomic seated play support, and optional pickup/strap accommodations.
historical_stance: Historically informed rather than literal reconstruction. Preserve ancient Greek architecture and material language while being explicit about modernized string count, hidden structure, ergonomics, and repeatable CAD.
governing_model: string instrument with near-equal speaking lengths; pitch primarily controlled by tension, gauge, and material
constraints: Do not clone the LUTHIEROS reference instrument.
Do not use the obsolete lyre-harp image as CAD authority.
Support future variants with more or fewer strings and larger or smaller body sizes.
Keep all main CAD dimensions equation-driven.
desired_outputs: print packet and build-log fallback files
